cards.front: What are the three groups of Spanish demonstratives?
cards.back: este/esta (near speaker), ese/esa (near listener), aquel/aquella (far from both)
cards.grammarNote: Spanish has a three-way distance system unlike English's two-way (this/that). Each group agrees in gender and number with the noun it modifies.
Este (m.), esta (f.), estos (m.pl.), estas (f.pl.) — used for things near the speaker.
The neuter forms (esto, eso, aquello) are used when referring to unknown things or abstract ideas. They never change form.

cards.front: that restaurant (near you) / that bus stop
cards.back: ese restaurante / esa parada de autobús
cards.grammarNote: Ese (m.), esa (f.), esos (m.pl.), esas (f.pl.) — for things nearer to the listener than to the speaker.

cards.front: I don't like that. (referring to an idea)
that building over there / that tower over there
cards.grammarNote: Aquel (m.), aquella (f.), aquellos (m.pl.), aquellas (f.pl.) — for things far from both speaker and listener. Often used for things you can just barely see or point to in the distance.

cards.front: That building over there is the museum.
Is that the bus stop? (near listener)
The taxi is on this side of the street.
cards.back: El taxi está en este lado de la calle.
